items.rationale: The house's central conviction: decentralization is what changes the risk profile of tokens, counterbalances Big Tech/AI concentration, and is the precondition for everything else they fund. Treated as entering a 'new era' with maturing best practices.
items.age_note: Held consistently 2022-2024 and intensifying; framed as a 'new era' by Dec 2023

items.rationale: Sees restaking as unbundling security from the EVM, creating a two-sided market that extends Ethereum's capabilities and enables ~100x faster infra innovation. Their single most enthusiastic recent bet.

Views a rollup-centric Ethereum as the scaling endgame; most excited that L2s unlock permissionless architecture innovation, with shared sequencers solving cross-rollup fragmentation. Reinforced by near-term EIP-4844 progress.
Believes privacy is a hard prerequisite for mainstream adoption and broader DeFi/identity utility; also funding ZK proving-system breakthroughs and pushing ZK to Bitcoin.
Frames decentralized blockchains as the counterbalancing force to centralized AI: permissionless compute/data markets to democratize ML, plus crypto provenance as the defense against AI deepfakes.
items.age_note: Recurring since 2022, sharpened into a concrete thesis by mid-2023 (Gensyn) and Dec 2023

items.rationale: Sees NFTs evolving from ownership records into composable digital brands, and a programmable onchain IP/rights layer as a major unlock for remix economies and creator value capture.
items.age_note: Shifted from pure-collectible framing (2022) to 'digital brands + IP layer' framing (2023-2024)

items.rationale: Excited about games where assets are user-owned and game contracts are composable building blocks, enabling permissionless modding and player agency.
Believes blockchains can coordinate real-world resources — compute, energy, transportation, wireless — wherever verification cost is below incumbent margin.
Views a private, decentralized personhood + sybil-resistance layer as a near-necessity as AI content floods the internet; pairs with trust/attestation graphs.
Treats a multi-chain future as a given and prizes interoperability infra that decouples security from liveness across chains.

Sees key primitives 'snapping into focus' (cheaper L2s, embedded wallets, alt-DA, easy app dev) as the historical accelerant for a developer/adoption wave; prioritizes converting passive owners into active onchain users.

The dominant house anxiety: an SEC that they believe refuses guidance, weaponizes uncertainty, and (under Gensler) aims to ban the technology — making good-faith building risky and breeding inconsistent court outcomes.
items.age_note: Sustained 2022-2024; escalated to 'endgame is banning' rhetoric by mid-2023, still central in the March 2024 Coinbase ruling analysis

items.rationale: Increasingly vocal that meaningless speculation, 100x-leverage perps and airdrop farming are the primary force holding the space back and distracting from real value creation.
items.age_note: Hardened sharply in late 2023-early 2024 amid the memecoin run

items.rationale: Warns that short/chunky lockups and fixed token allocations exist to let VCs dump on retail, exacerbate the FDV problem, and misalign incentives; pushes 4yr/1yr-cliff standards and dilutable allocations tied to decentralization milestones.
Fears bad/absent US policy drives development offshore, ceding ground to China and undermining US oversight — explicitly framed as national security and analogized to semiconductors.
Concerned that harmful MEV extraction is a real, large rent on users; skeptical it is 'inevitable' and backs faster blocks, order-flow auctions and fair-ordering (Prof) to reduce it.

Research shows flight-to-safety across stablecoins amplifies run risk on the weakest; house aligns with regulators against algorithmic stablecoins and emphasizes reserve/collateral quality over peg mechanism.
items.age_note: Crystallized post-Terra; settled into 'fiat-backed/decentralized good, algorithmic bad' by 2023

items.rationale: Acknowledges the 'modularity paradox' — proliferating rollups fragment liquidity, interoperability and security, and add single points of failure; the motivation for shared sequencing bets.
Notes a single staking entity controlling more than a third of staked ETH as a concentration risk, favoring client-specific configurability to manage it.
items.age_note: Stated Oct 2023, not heavily restated since

items.rationale: Flags that quantum computers would break today's signatures (and slightly advantage PoW miners); defensible with post-quantum schemes but a ~15-year watch item with large blockchain-state costs.
items.age_note: Research view from mid-2023 (external/research framing), not a frequently restated house priority

items.rationale: Treated as the bedrock asset: secures a multi-hundred-billion-dollar economy, has a credible rollup scaling roadmap, and the house argues its legal status as a commodity (not a security) was effectively conceded when ETH futures were approved.

Respected as a technically superior store of value and the most battle-tested chain; house is excited about making it programmable via a VERIFY_SNARK opcode — but flags a long-term security-model concern as block rewards trend to fees.
items.age_note: Bullish as digital gold (2023); paired with explicit long-run security-budget concern ('security model needs to change')

items.rationale: Viewed as comparatively safe and transparent; benefits from flight-to-safety during stress and the house argues it is likely not a security. Preferred over opaque alternatives.

Flagged for opaque reserve quality; during market stress the house's own research shows traders dump the 'safer' coins into USDT for regulatory-arbitrage reasons while doubting Tether's reserve transparency — a latent run/quality risk.

Cited repeatedly as the canonical failure: endogenous-collateral 'death spiral,' undercollateralized, not decentralized, and the SEC's straightforward Howey win. Held up as the warning against algorithmic designs and hyped yields.
items.age_note: Negative since 2022 and fully vindicated by the Dec 2023 Terraform ruling — historical, but still actively invoked as a lesson

risk_regime.rationale: As of late March 2024 the house is structurally bullish on building: their State of Crypto Index is at an all-time high, they describe key infrastructure primitives 'snapping into focus,' and they tell founders there has 'never been a better time to build.' Crucially, this is risk-on on fundamentals (infra, decentralization, real applications) while being explicitly risk-OFF on speculation — they actively distance the house from memecoins, leverage and 'casino' behavior. So the posture is conviction in technology and adoption, paired with disdain for the froth, and a persistent overhang of US regulatory hostility that tempers (but does not reverse) the optimism.

notes: Method: a16z team tweets and official investment announcements are weighted as 'house view'; the dated research/conference transcripts are frequently EXTERNAL guest speakers (Avalanche, Chia/Bram Cohen, Arweave, Compound's Leshner, academic cryptographers) and are treated as context, not consensus, unless echoed by team members — e.g., Avalanche subnet enthusiasm (2023-02-03) is a sponsored guest and is NOT counted as house view. Key recency-driven reversals/evolutions: (1) The team decisively cooled on the 2017-era ICO/'utility token' model — by 2022-2023 they treat it as settled-dead and pro-decentralization ('I don't want to return to the ICO days'), a stance unchanged through 2024. (2) Anti-speculation sentiment hardened materially in late 2023-early 2024 (memecoins), moving from mild caution to calling the casino 'the primary force holding the space back.' (3) NFT framing migrated from collectibles/scarcity toward 'digital brands + programmable onchain IP.' (4) On regulation, nuance grew: the team distinguishes the SEC (viewed as hostile/regulation-by-enforcement) from the CFTC (graded as more principled, Oct 2023) and increasingly supports targeted legislation (FIT/market-structure) over pure opposition. Stable bedrock convictions across the whole window: decentralization, composability, and 'regulate apps not protocols.' Token-level price views are sparse in this corpus (the team is thesis/legal/research-oriented, not making price calls); ETH/BTC/stablecoin entries reflect qualitative posture, not targets. Conviction scores are intensity, not probabilities.
